name: Chilli Mash
subtitle: The building block of Sriracha and Sambal
blrNote: Go to KR Market and buy a kilo of the bright red "Byadgi" chillies for flavor or the small "Guntur" ones for pure heat.
tldr: Pulverize chillies with salt. Let them rot into a complex, fruity, spicy paste.

steps.title: Blend
steps.instruction: Remove stems. Pulse chillies in a blender with salt until you have a coarse mash.

steps.title: Jar
steps.instruction: Pack into a jar. Use a weight if possible, otherwise stir every few days to prevent mould on top.

steps.title: Age
steps.instruction: Let it sit for at least a week. The flavor will move from "raw heat" to "fruity complexity."

images.caption: Chilli Mash
images.alt: Chilli Mash — The building block of Sriracha and Sambal

culturalContext.story: This is how Tabasco is made—they age their chilli mash in oak barrels for three years.
culturalContext.significance: Fermentation breaks down the capsaicin slightly but adds layers of flavor that vinegar-based sauces lack.
culturalContext.funFact: The liquid that rises to the top is "Chilli Whey"—use it to marinate chicken.
